title: Power distribution assembly with boltless busbar system
abstract: A power distribution assembly with boltless busbar system for electrically and mechanically connecting a component to a power source is disclosed. The power distribution assembly includes an interconnecting busbar segment that connects two boltless busbar system together. Each boltless busbar system includes a male busbar assembly and a female busbar assembly. The male busbar assembly includes a male terminal, internal spring member, internal male housing, and external male housing that receives an extent of the male terminal, the internal spring member, and the internal male housing. This male terminal includes a side wall arrangement that defines a receiver and further includes at least one contact arm. An internal spring member with at least one spring arm resides within the male terminal receiver. The female busbar assembly includes a female terminal with a receptacle that is dimensioned to receive both the male terminal and the spring member in a connected position to secure the female busbar assembly to the male busbar assembly.
claims: 1. A busbar system configured to electrically connect a component within a power distribution system, the busbar system comprising: a male busbar assembly including: (i) a male terminal, and (ii) an external male housing having a busbar coupling projection and a side wall arrangement that defines an external male housing receiver, wherein the external male housing receiver receives the male terminal; and a female busbar assembly including: (i) a conductor, (ii) a female terminal formed in the conductor and having a receiver, said receiver dimensioned to receive both the male terminal in a connected position to secure the female busbar assembly to the male busbar assembly, and (iii) a busbar coupling hole formed in the conductor and configured to receive an extent of the busbar coupling projection in the connected position.
